title: Document the new operator-vs-template skill split
description: Revise architecture, onboarding, and developer docs so they describe operator-facing repo-root skills in `.agents/skills/`, subagent skill templates in `templates/skills/`, and unchanged execution-root Codex discovery under `exec_root/.agents/skills/`.
acceptance_criteria: Docs no longer claim the full subagent Codex catalog lives in repo-root `.agents/skills/`.
Docs clearly distinguish repository source paths from execution-root discovery paths for Codex subagents.
Any explicit template or contributor references to repo-root subagent-only skills are updated to the new `templates/skills/` location.

handoff_summary.completed: Added templates/skills/ to the CLAUDE.md project layout and corrected .agents/skills/ description to "operator overrides only". Updated docs/onboarding.md directory structure, git-commit table, and post-init ownership section to distinguish templates/skills/ (primary subagent catalog) from .agents/skills/ (operator exceptions). Fixed templates/agents/tester.md skill path reference from .agents/skills/ to templates/skills/.
handoff_summary.remaining: AGENTS.md (outside bead scope) retains the stale "Shared skill catalog" label at line 61. A follow-up bead should update it or the operator can edit it directly per the no-manual-code-changes exception.
handoff_summary.risks: Low. All changes are documentation-only. The stale AGENTS.md reference is cosmetic — no runtime behavior depends on it.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — all changes are in documentation files with no overlap with other in-progress beads.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: docs/development.md and docs/multi-backend-agents.md required no changes — both already accurately described the split between templates/skills/ (primary) and .agents/skills/ (operator overrides). Only files that contained inaccurate or stale references were modified.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: AGENTS.md line 61 still reads ".agents/skills/     Shared skill catalog" — this file was not in the bead's expected_files scope so it was not modified. It should be addressed in a follow-up or by the operator directly.

conflict_risks: None — all changes are in documentation files with no overlap with other in-progress beads.

execution_history.summary: Updated CLAUDE.md, docs/onboarding.md, and templates/agents/tester.md to document the operator-vs-template skill split; development.md and multi-backend-agents.md were already accurate.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
